Compilation/tests were not run in this batch because they are scoped to T2.","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx"],"commands_run":["sed -n '1,240p' src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx","rg --files | rg 'config-utils\\.ts$'","rg -n \"parseResolution|getTimelineDurationInFrames\" src/tools/video-editor -g '*.ts' -g '*.tsx'","sed -n '1,120p' src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts","sed -n '1,190p' src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx","git diff -- src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx"]}],"sense_check_acknowledgments":[{"sense_check_id":"SC1","executor_note":"Confirmed in the updated `ResizeObserver` callback that `isTimelineMaximized` branches to `MIN_PREVIEW_HEIGHT`, the non-maximized path uses the aspect-ratio-aware preview height, and the effect dependency array includes `aspectRatio` and `isTimelineMaximized`."}]}

item.text: `tsx` hit a sandbox IPC restriction. I’m switching to a direct Node loader path for the throwaway script so the verification still runs without changing the repo.

{"output":"Completed T2 verification. TypeScript compilation passed, the full `src/tools/video-editor` Vitest module passed, and the throwaway threshold-reproduction script confirmed the expected landscape/portrait/floor behavior before being deleted. Best-effort checkpointing to `.megaplan/plans/make-the-video-editor-switch-20260406-0741/execution_batch_2.json` was not possible because the file was not writable.","files_changed":[],"commands_run":["cat package.json","rg --files src/tools/video-editor | rg '\\.(test|spec)\\.(ts|tsx)$'","test -w .megaplan/plans/make-the-video-editor-switch-20260406-0741/execution_batch_2.json && echo writable || echo not_writable","git status --short","npx tsc --noEmit","npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor","sed -n '1,220p' tsconfig.json","sed -n '1,220p' tsconfig.app.json","npx tsx verify-condensed-threshold.ts","node --import tsx/esm verify-condensed-threshold.ts","git diff -- src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx"],"deviations":["Checkpoint file `.megaplan/plans/make-the-video-editor-switch-20260406-0741/execution_batch_2.json` was not writable, so no batch checkpoint was written.","`npx tsx verify-condensed-threshold.ts` failed in the sandbox with `EPERM` on its IPC pipe; the same script was then run successfully with `node --import tsx/esm verify-condensed-threshold.ts` and deleted afterward."],"task_updates":[{"task_id":"T2","status":"done","executor_notes":"Verification passed. `npx tsc --noEmit` exited 0. The full video-editor test module passed with `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor` (`32` test files, `127` tests, `0` failures). A throwaway script importing `parseResolution` reproduced the threshold math and confirmed: `1920x1080 @ 1200px -> availablePreviewWidth 804, minPreviewHeight 452.25, threshold 684.25`; `1080x1920 @ 1200px -> availablePreviewWidth 804, minPreviewHeight 1429.33, threshold 1661.33`; `1920x1080 @ 500px -> availablePreviewWidth 104, minPreviewHeight 180, threshold 412`; `<396px width -> availablePreviewWidth 0, minPreviewHeight 180, threshold 412`. The script also asserted that portrait threshold exceeds landscape threshold and that the `MIN_PREVIEW_HEIGHT=180` floor is never violated.
